title: Ipomopsis tenuifolia
taxonomy: Ipomopsis tenuifolia is a species of flowering plant in the evening primrose family, Polemoniaceae. This plant is native to North America and is sometimes referred to as the "naked poppy". Its classification is as follows: it is a member of the genus Ipomopsis, which is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in the Americas. The genus Ipomopsis is further classified under the family Polemoniaceae, and the order Polemoniales. This classification indicates its evolutionary relationships and its place within the plant kingdom.

intro: Ipomopsis tenuifolia, also known as scarlet gilia or skyrocket, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its bright red to pink flowers and tall, upright stems. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote respiratory health, and its extracts have shown potential in reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, likely due to its bioactive constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and coumarins. The bioactive compounds in Ipomopsis tenuifolia may contribute to its therapeutic effects, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. In herbal medicine, Ipomopsis tenuifolia is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and colds.
